text.title: OKR Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: SaaS product and solo creator OKR sets with full O/KR structure, and concrete antipatterns.

example.note: O1: Achieve product-market fit. KR1: increase "very disappointed" survey response from 25% to 40%. KR2: improve Day-30 retention from 20% to 35%. KR3: reach 100 paying customers with less than 10% monthly churn.
O2: Build scalable acquisition engine. KR1: achieve 5,000 monthly organic visitors. KR2: reduce CAC from $200 to $100. KR3: maintain content pipeline of 12 blog posts published.
O3: Delight early adopters. KR1: NPS score of 50+. KR2: average support response time under 4 hours. KR3: 10 customer testimonials collected.
O1: Establish newsletter as primary channel. KR1: grow subscriber list from 500 to 2,000. KR2: maintain 45%+ open rate. KR3: convert 50 subscribers to paid product.
O2: Launch first digital product. KR1: complete and launch course by [date]. KR2: generate $5,000 in first month. KR3: achieve 4.5+ star rating from first 20 customers.

antipattern.description: Key Results that are tasks: "Publish 12 blog posts", "Launch feature X", "Run 5 user interviews".
antipattern.reason: Tasks measure output, not outcome. Completing the task does not prove the Objective was reached. Rewrite as the outcome the task enables: "Achieve 5,000 monthly organic visitors" instead of "Publish 12 blog posts".

antipattern.description: Too many OKRs: 7+ Objectives, each with 5 KRs.
antipattern.reason: Exceeding the 3–5 Objective cap destroys focus. With 7 Objectives, none gets the sustained attention needed to move metrics. Everything is equally urgent means nothing is actually prioritised.

antipattern.description: Setting 100% confidence targets to guarantee achievement.
antipattern.reason: Sandbagged targets are not OKRs — they are delivery commitments with extra branding. A 100% score every quarter signals that targets need recalibration upward, not celebration.

antipattern.description: Setting OKRs at the start of the quarter and not reviewing until the end.
antipattern.reason: Blocked KRs stay blocked without weekly visibility. Weekly check-ins catch blockers at week 3, not week 12.
A KR without a starting point is unmeasurable. "NPS of 50+" without knowing current NPS cannot be scored. Always establish the baseline before setting the target.
